species.citation: (Blanco) Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 27 (1905) 24 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 57; Kosterm., Reinwardtia 1 (1950) 43; Raganas et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 1193; ≡ Heritiera tinctoria Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 653. = Pterocymbium macrocrater Warb. , Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 117 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 57.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. BABUYAN ISLS, CAMIGUIN, LAGEN, LEYTE (photos), LUZON: Apayao (photos), Bataan, Batangas (photos), Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Pampanga, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon (photos), Davao Oriental (photos), Davao del Norte, Misamis Oriental (photos), MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Widespread in Luzon from at least Cagayan to Camarines Sur; in secondary or thin primary forests at sea level and low and medium elevation. 0m to 50-100m (photos).

Life Sc. 10 (2021) 439; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 990; Raganas et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 1193. = Sterculia glabrifolia Merr. , PJS 17 (1920) 283 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 54. = Sterculia luzonica Warb. , Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 115 ; Merr., Fl, Manila (1912) 327.
species.distribution: Borneo, Moluccas, Philippines, Sulawesi, Taiwan. APO, BATAN, BILIRAN, BORACAY (photos), DANJUGAN (photos), GUIMARAS, HAMORAON, HOMONHON, LUZON: Aurora (photos), Camarines, Camarines Sur, Isabela, NCR, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Davao del Norte, Surigao, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PAMALICAN, PANAY (photos), PUJADA, ROMBLON, SIARGAO, SIBUYAN, SIDANAO, SULU ARCHIPELAGO, TABLAS, TAWI-TAWI, TICAO, TURTLE IS.
species.notes: Widespread in W Luzon and Mindanao; coastal thickets and forests on sandy clay or coralline limestone, usually immediately back of the beach. 0-100m.

species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. BILIRAN, BUCAS GRANDE, LUZON: Batangas, Camarines, Laguna, Nueva Ecija, Quezon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, Surigao del Norte, Zamboanga del Norte (photos), Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Thickets and forests at low elevation.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 1008 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 327; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 53; Tantra, Lemb. Pen. Hut. Comm. (1976) 102; Cañeda et al., Davao Research J. 16 (2025) 91.
species.distribution: Africa to India through Malesia to Australia. APULIT, BALABAC, BORACAY (photos), BUYAYAO (photos), CORREGIDOR (photos), CULION, DINAGAT, LUZON: Abra, Bataan, Bulacan, Cagayan, Cavite, Ilocos Norte, Laguna, NCR, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan (photos), Quezon, Rizal, Tarlac, MARINDUQUE, MINDANAO: Davao Occidental, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY, SIBUYAN, TABLAS.
species.notes: Luzon (Cagayan) to Palawan and Mindanao, in most or all islands and provinces. Deciduous tree in semi-open forests, especially in very seasonal areas, particularly common along the sea coast on coralline limestone and sandstone. 0-1000m.

species.citation: Vent. , Jard. Malmaison (1805) t.91 ; Tantra, Lemb. Pen. Hut. Comm. (1976) 102; Buhay et al., Biodiversitas 26 (2025) 3087. = Sterculia crassiramea Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 29 (1905) 28 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 53.

species.notes: Deciduous tree in primary forests at low and medium elevation. 400m (photos).

species.citation: Tantra , Revis. Gen. Sterculia Malesia (1976) 102; Type: PNH 18812 Conklin (L, holo), Mindoro: Mindoro Oriental (Mansalay, Mt Yagaw, known only from the type collection).

species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. species.citation: Vent. , Jard. Malmaison (1805) t.91 ; Tantra, Lemb. Pen. Hut. Comm. (1976) 102. = Sterculia brevipetiolata Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 35 (1906) 40 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 53. = Sterculia cuneata R.Br. , Pl. Jav. Rar. (Bennett) (1844) 232 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 328; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 53. = Sterculia ferruginea R.Br. , Pl. Jav. Rar. (Bennett) (1844) 232 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 54. = Sterculia humilis Elmer , LPB 2 (1910) 681 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 54. = Sterculia obovata Merr. , Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 29 (1905) 28 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 55. = Sterculia stipularis R.Br. , Pl. Jav. Rar. (Bennett) (1844) 232 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 56. var. rubiginosa. Distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand. BASILAN, CATANDUANES, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Bataan, Benguet, Cagayan, Camarines, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MANICANI, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, POLILLO, TAWI-TAWI. Notes: Occupying a wide spectrum of habitat ranging from thickets and open secondary forests to primary forests. 0-1600m. Native. var. divaricata (Merr.) Tantra, Revis. Gen. Sterculia Malesia (1976) 102; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 13; ≡ Sterculia divaricata Merr., PJS 8c (1913) 384; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 54. Distribution: Borneo, Philippines. HOMONHON, LEYTE, LUZON: Bataan, Benguet, PALAWAN, SAMAR. Notes: Primary forests at low elevation. Native. var. setistipula (Merr.) Tantra, Revis. Gen. Sterculia Malesia (1976) 102; ≡ Sterculia setistipula Merr., Univ. Calif. Publ. Bot. 15 (1929) 192. = Sterculia multistipularis Elmer, LPB 2 (1908) 497; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 55. = Sterculia longisepala Merr., PJS 30 (1926) 409; Type: BS 44070 Ramos & Edaño (NY, US, iso), Tawi-tawi. Distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines. LEYTE, LUZON: Benguet, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, TAWI-TAWI. Notes: Forested ravines up to 1200m. Native.

species.notes: Forested dry slopes at low elevation.

species.citation: Korth. , Ned. Kruidk. Arch. 1 (1848) 306 ; Tantra, Lemb. Pen. Hut. Comm. (1976) 102. var. jagori (Warb.) Tantra, Revis. Gen. Sterculia Malesia (1976) 102; ≡ Sterculia jagori Warb., Fragm. Fl. Philipp. (1904) 115; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 55; Type: Jagor 1004, Leyte. = Sterculia membranifolia Kosterm., Reinwardtia 1 (1950) 49. = Pterocymbium gigantifolium Elmer, LPB 1 (1908) 320; Type: Elmer 9424 (L, iso), Leyte, near Palo, Jan-1906. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. BASILAN, CEBU (photos), LEYTE, LUZON: Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Davao, Misamis Oriental, SAMAR, SIARGAO. Notes: Primary forests at low elevation. Native.
species.distribution: APULIT, BASILAN, BOHOL, CAMIGUIN, CEBU (photos), LEYTE, LUZON: Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Davao, Davao del Norte, Misamis Oriental, South Cotabato (photos), Surigao del Norte (photos), Zamboanga Sibugay, MINDORO, PALAWAN, SAMAR, SIARGAO.

species.notes: Note by P.B. Pelser (21-June-20): Ridsdale et al. ISU517 (L) from Isabela Prov possibly represents this species.
